observations.summary: Some people respond to grief by discarding all reminders of their loss, attempting to start anew with nothing.

evidence.quote: Some people throw everything away when they lose the thing they love most—burn it all, scatter the ashes, start fresh with empty hands.

observations.summary: Others respond to grief by clinging tightly to every fragment of the past, to the point of suffocation.

evidence.quote: Others keep every thread, every cracked vessel, every scrap of parchment with nothing written on it, and pull it tighter and tighter until there is no room left to breathe.

observations.summary: Aviel is characterized as someone who clings tightly to memories or remnants of the past, unable to let go.
observations.notes: Refers to the previous description of clinging tightly to remnants.

observations.summary: Aviel's son is actively trying to undo his father's tendency to cling tightly to the past.

evidence.quote: Which is relevant because it is exactly what his son was creeping through the dark to undo.

observations.notes: Indicates a conflict or mission related to grief and letting go.

accepted_observations.notes: Refers to the previous description of clinging tightly to remnants.
